zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elizabeth Farrar於1698年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福時，她的父親Richard Farr13歲，母親Mary Perrie9歲。她於1719年與William James Melton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福韓諾福結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1758年在她的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Elizabeth Farrar于1698年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福时，她的父亲Richard Farr13岁，母亲Mary Perrie9岁。她于1719年与William James Melton在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福韓諾福结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1758年在她的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Farrar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): occupational name for a blacksmith or worker in iron. From Middle English fer(r)o(u)r, fer(r)er, fa(r)ro(u)r ‘ironworker, blacksmith’ (Old French ferreor, from medieval Latin ferrator, an agent derivative of ferrare ‘to shoe horses’, from ferrum ‘iron’, in medieval Latin ‘horseshoe’). Compare Ferrier and Farrow .

variants.name: Ferrer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Juan, Jorge, Carlos, Angel, Pedro, Roberto, Manuel, Ramon.
Catalan and southern French: from Latin ferrarius ‘ironworker, blacksmith’, either an occupational name or from the same word as an old personal name. This is the commonest Catalan surname.
English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Farrer
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
German: variant of   Forer   or   Fahrer  .

variants.name: Ferrar
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .

variants.name: Ferro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Angelo, Mario, Pasquale, Alberto, Antonino, Bernardo, Guido, Carmine, Concetta, Dino, Ottavio, Pietro, Rocco.  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Carlos, Ramon, Eduardo, Fermin, Sixto, Angel.

Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, and Maltese: from ferro ‘iron’ (from Latin ferrum), used as a nickname denoting someone who was strong or as a metonymic occupational name for someone who produced or worked iron.
Italian: from a medieval personal name, preserved in the modern pet form Ferruccio.

variants.name: Fara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Eduardo, Carlos, Mario.
Italian (Sardinia): occupational name from Sardinian fara ‘messenger, courier, collector’.
Czech and Slovak (also Fára): topographic name from fara ‘priest's house’.

variants.name: Farrow
meanings: English (northern): hypercorrected form of Farrar , an occupational name for a smith or worker in iron. The original -ar or -er ending of this name came to be regarded as an error, and was changed to -ow.

variants.name: Farrah
meanings: English: variant of   Farrar  .
Americanized form of Arabic   Farah  .

variants.name: Ferrier
meanings: Scottish and English: occupational name for a smith, one who shoed horses, from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, ironworker’ (medieval Latin ferrarius, from ferrus ‘horseshoe’, from Latin ferrum ‘iron’). Compare Farrier , Farrar .

Scottish and English: occupational name for a ferryman, from Middle English ferier(e) ‘ferryman’. Black reports that lands called Ferrylands in Dumbarton, by a ferry across the Clyde, belonged to Robert Ferrier in 1512.
French: occupational name from Old French ferrier ‘blacksmith, farrier’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Farra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ammar, Bassel, Fadi, Fadia, Farid, Hazem, Nabil, Nadim, Salem, Samir, Waseem, Yasser.  French Henri, Marcel.
English: variant of   Farrar  .
Arabic (the Levant): possibly a variant of   Farah  . Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians.

variants.name: Furrow
meanings: Probably an altered form of English Farrow (see Farrar ).

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elizabeth Farrar was born in 1698, in Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Richard Farr, was 13 and her mother, Mary Perrie, was 9. She married William James Melton in 1719, in Hanover, Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1758, in her hometown, at the age of 60.

Elle a épousé William James Melton en 1719, à Hanover, Hanover, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1758, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Elizabeth Farrar è nata il nel 1698 nel Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America, sua padre, Richard Farr, aveva 13 anni e sua madre, Mary Perrie, aveva 9 anni. Lei sposato William James Melton nel 1719, nel Hanover, Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1758, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Farrarが1698年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハノバーで生まれた時，父Richard Farrは13歳，母Mary Perrieは9歳であった。1719年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハノバーハノバーでWilliam James Meltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1758年に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth Farrar(이)가 1698년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 핸버에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Farr(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Perrie(은)는 9세였다. 그녀는 1719년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 핸버 핸버에서 William James Melton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1758년 그녀의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Elizabeth Farrar родилась в 1698 г. в местности хановир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Richard Farr было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Perrie, 9. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William James Melton в 1719 г. в местности хановир, хановир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1758 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Elizabeth Farrar nació en 1698, en Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Farr, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Mary Perrie, tenía 9 años. Se casó con William James Melton en 1719, en Hanover, Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1758, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años.
